translations.english: Meet Karliah at the Ragged Flagon
Learn the Word of Power from Einarth
translations.chinese: 从爱纳斯处学习力量之语

translations.english: The hunt is on and I'm on my way to Riftweald Manor, Mercer Frey's estate in Riften, to locate a clue as to his current whereabouts.
translations.chinese: 狩猎已经开始，我前往墨瑟·弗雷在裂谷城的庄园——裂野庄园，寻找关于他下落的线索。
（可选）向小烦打听沃德的情况

translations.english: (Optional) Shoot the mechanism to lower the ramp
I've discovered some plans in Riftweald Manor. This must be where Mercer Frey is headed. I should bring these the Brynjolf as soon as possible.
translations.chinese: 我在裂野庄园里发现了一些计划书。这一定是墨瑟·弗雷要去的地方。我应该尽快把这些东西送给布林乔夫。

translations.english: I'm on my way back to the Ragged Flagon to meet up with Karliah. Using the information obtained from Gallus's journal as proof, I hope to convince Brynjolf of Karliah's innocence and Mercer Frey's betrayal.
translations.chinese: 我在回漏壶的路上碰到了卡莱亚。以加卢斯的日记为证据，希望布林乔夫能够相信卡莱亚是无辜的，而墨瑟·弗雷背叛了他们。

translations.english: Search Morvunskar for Sam and the Staff
translations.chinese: 在莫万斯卡寻找山姆和法杖

translations.english: Return the wedding ring to Ysolda in Whiterun
translations.chinese: 将婚戒还给白漫城的伊索尔达

translations.english: Talk to Ysolda in Whiterun about the staff
translations.chinese: 问白漫城的伊索尔达关于法杖的事

translations.english: Ask about Sam and the staff in Rorikstead
translations.chinese: 在洛利克镇询问山姆与法杖的事

translations.english: Apologize to the priestesses of Dibella
Help clean up the Temple of Dibella
"Sam" turned out to be the daedric prince of debauchery, Sanguine. He spurred me on during a drunken night of revelry that crossed all of Skyrim. I've acquired the Sanguine Rose for my troubles.
translations.chinese: “山姆”原来是迪德拉放荡君王，桑吉恩。他在一个醉酒狂欢的夜晚不断驱策我。而我也得到了血腥玫瑰作为麻烦的补偿。

translations.english: I have awoken from a night of debauchery with no idea what happened. Sam and the staff he promised me have disappeared. Ysolda in Whiterun has told me to look for Sam in Morvunskar.
translations.chinese: 我从一夜放荡中醒来却不知发生了什么。山姆和他答应给我的法杖都不见了。白漫城的伊索尔达让我去莫万斯卡找山姆。

translations.english: I have awoken from a night of debauchery with no idea what happened. Sam and the staff he promised me have disappeared. A note of debt I gave to Ennis in Rorikstead has directed me to Ysolda in Whiterun, she might be able to direct me to Sam.
translations.chinese: 我从一夜放荡中醒来却不知发生了什么。山姆和他答应给我的法杖都不见了。我在洛利克镇留给埃尼斯的字条上提到了要去找白漫城的伊索尔达。她也许能帮我找到山姆。

translations.english: I have awoken from a night of debauchery with no idea what happened. Sam and the staff he promised me have disappeared. Ennis in Rorikstead will tell me more about what happened once I return the goat I stole from him.
translations.chinese: 我从一夜放荡中醒来却不知发生了什么。山姆和他答应给我的法杖都不见了。等我把偷走的羊还给洛利克镇的埃尼斯，他就会告诉我更多情况。

translations.english: I have awoken from a night of debauchery with no idea what happened. Sam and the staff he promised me have disappeared. The priestesses of Dibella have told me to ask about Sam in Rorikstead.
translations.chinese: 我从一夜放荡中醒来却不知发生了什么。山姆和他答应给我的法杖都不见了。迪贝拉祭司让我去洛利克镇打听山姆。

translations.english: I have awoken in the Temple Of Dibella in Markarth. Sam and the staff he promised me have disappeared. I have no idea how I got here but a priestess has told me I trashed the Temple. The priestess might tell me what happened to Sam and the staff if I pick up the place.
translations.chinese: 我在马卡斯的迪贝拉神庙醒来。山姆和他答应给我的法杖都不见了。我不知道怎么到这里来的，但一个祭司说我弄乱了神庙。如果我把这里收拾好，祭司可能会告诉我山姆和法杖的事。

translations.english: I have awoken in the Temple Of Dibella in Markarth. Sam and the staff he promised me have disappeared.
translations.chinese: 我在马卡斯的迪贝拉神庙醒来。山姆和他答应给我的法杖都不见了。

translations.english: Speak to Arngeir for further training
translations.chinese: 与艾恩盖尔谈谈进一步的训练

translations.english: Learn the Word of Power from Borri

The Greybeards summoned me to their monastery of High Hrothgar on the slopes of the Throat of the World. Their leader, Arngeir, recognized me as "Dragonborn", and taught me more about my new Voice powers. After passing several tests, they sent me on one final trial, retrieving the legendary Horn of Jurgen Windcaller.
translations.chinese: 灰胡子将我召唤到了世界之喉高坡上的霍斯加修道院。他们的领袖艾恩盖尔认为我是“龙裔”，并教会了更多关于吼声力量的知识。在几番测验后，他们派我进行最后一项试炼，取回传说中“唤风者”约根的号角。
与乌弗瑞克·风暴斗篷交谈

translations.english: The Jarl of Whiterun won't help me capture a dragon as long as the civil war is raging. If I can get the Greybeards to host a peace council, I can try to convince the Imperials and the Stormcloaks to agree to a truce in order to defeat Alduin, who is a threat to both sides in the civil war.
translations.chinese: 白漫领主拒绝在内战持续的情况下帮助我捉龙。如果我能让灰胡子主持一次和平会议，就能说服帝国和风暴斗篷同意休战以击败奥杜因。奥杜因对于内战双方都是威胁。

translations.english: Brynjolf has been convinced of Karliah's innocence and Mercer's betrayal after investigating the Guild's treasure vault and finding it empty. Of greater mystery is the fact that the vault can only be opened with two keys and is utterly impervious to lockpicking. How Mercer Frey could have breached it is a mystery to us all.
translations.chinese: 布林乔夫调查了公会的金库，金库已经被搬空了，他相信了卡莱亚是清白的，而墨瑟确实叛变了。更神秘的是，金库只能用两把钥匙同时打开，而且完全不受开锁技术的影响。我们都不知道墨瑟·弗雷是如何破解的。

translations.english: Ask Falk Firebeard about Wolfskull Cave
translations.chinese: 向“火胡”佛可问问狼头骨洞穴的事

translations.english: I have confronted and stopped a group of necromancers attempting to summon Potema, the Wolf Queen, in Wolfskull Cave. I should return to Falk Firebeard with the news.
translations.chinese: 我在狼头骨洞穴遇到并阻止了一群试图召唤狼后波特玛的死灵法师。我该带着这个消息回去找“火胡”佛可。

translations.english: Kill the bandit leader located at <Alias=BountyLocation>
Bring a Dragon Scale and a Dragon Bone to Esbern
translations.chinese: 将一个龙鳞和一个龙骨带给伊思本

translations.english: Thieves stolen Finn's Lute from the Bards College over a year ago. Inge Six-Fingers has asked me to recover the lute from their stronghold.
translations.chinese: 一年前，盗贼从吟游诗人学院偷走了芬恩的鲁特琴。“六指”因格要我把琴从他们的要塞里取回来。

translations.english: Giraud Gemane of the Bards College has found a clue to the location of the legendary drum of Rjorn. He's asked me to go to Halldir's Cairn and retrieve it.
translations.chinese: 吟游诗人学院的吉劳德·格玛尼找到了传说中荣恩的鼓所在的位置。他让我去霍迪尔石冢将鼓找回来。

translations.english: Receive a reading from Olava the Feeble
translations.chinese: 从“软脚虾”奥拉瓦处获得解读
找到关于墨瑟所在地的证据

translations.english: Bring Gleda the Goat back to Ennis in Rorikstead
translations.chinese: 将山羊格丽达送回到洛利克镇的埃尼斯处

translations.english: Find the wedding ring in Witchmist Grove
translations.chinese: 在女巫迷雾树林找到婚戒

translations.english: Brynjolf is convinced Mercer Frey intends to perform one final heist before departing Skyrim for good. According to the plans I discovered in Riftweald Manor, he's after the "Eyes of the Falmer," two gems of enormous value. If we have any hopes of intercepting Mercer, we need to proceed to their location immediately.
translations.chinese: 布林乔夫确信墨瑟·弗雷打算在永远离开天际前，进行最后一次抢劫。根据我在裂野庄园发现的计划，他的目标是两颗名为“伐莫之眼”的昂贵宝石。如果我们想截住墨瑟，就得马上前往宝石的所在地。

translations.english: On second thought, perhaps I'll invest another time.

I have awoken from a night of debauchery with no idea what happened. Sam and the staff he promised me have disappeared. Ysolda in Whiterun will give me more information about Same once I return the ring I borrowed from her to give to a hagraven Witchmist Grove.
translations.chinese: 我从一夜放荡中醒来却不知发生了什么。山姆和他答应给我的法杖都不见了。只要把交给女巫迷雾森林乌鸦鬼婆的戒指还给白漫城的伊索尔达，她会告诉我更多情况。

translations.english: I have awoken from a night of debauchery with no idea what happened. Sam and the staff he promised me have disappeared. Ysolda in Whiterun will give me more information about Same once I return the ring I borrowed from her to give to someone in Witchmist Grove.
translations.chinese: 我从一夜放荡中醒来却不知发生了什么。山姆和他答应给我的法杖都不见了。只要把交给女巫迷雾森林某人的戒指还给白漫城的伊索尔达，她会告诉我更多情况。

translations.english: The Greybeards have summoned me to their monastery of High Hrothgar on the slopes of the Throat of the World. They seem to have learned of the mysterious power that I gained when I killed the dragon outside Whiterun.
translations.chinese: 灰胡子将我召唤到了世界之喉高坡上的霍斯加修道院。他们好像知道了我在白漫城外杀龙时获得神秘力量的事。

translations.english: I have awoken in the Temple Of Dibella in Markarth. Sam and the staff he promised me have disappeared. I have no idea how I got here but a priestess has told me I trashed the Temple. I have cleaned up the Temple, if I apologize to the priestess she might tell me what happened to Sam and the staff.
translations.chinese: 我在马卡斯的迪贝拉神庙醒来。山姆和他答应给我的法杖都不见了。我不知道怎么到这里来的，但一个祭司说我弄乱了神庙。我已经打扫了神庙，如果我向祭司道歉，她会告诉我山姆和法杖的事。
将芬恩的鲁特琴交给因格

translations.english: I have awoken from a night of debauchery with no idea what happened. Sam and the staff he promised me have disappeared. Ennis in Rorikstead will tell me more about what happened once I return the goat I stole from him.
translations.chinese: 我从一夜放荡中醒来却不知发生了什么。山姆和他答应给我的法杖都不见了。等我把偷走的羊还给洛利克镇的埃尼斯，他就会告诉我更多情况。
梭默大使馆·爱琳温日光房
将荣恩的鼓交给吉劳德

translations.english: A representative from Dragon Bridge has appealed to the court in Solitude for help investigating Wolfskull Cave. Falk Firebeard has asked me to clear out the cave.
translations.chinese: 龙桥镇的代表向独孤城宫廷请求调查狼头骨洞穴。“火胡”佛可让我去把洞穴清理干净。

translations.english: A representative from Dragon Bridge has appealed to the court in Solitude for help investigating Wolfskull Cave. Falk Firebeard is looking for someone to look into the issue.
translations.chinese: 龙桥镇的代表向独孤城宫廷请求调查狼头骨洞穴。“火胡”佛可正在找人接手这个问题。

translations.english: The Jarl of Whiterun wouldn't help me capture a dragon as long as the civil war was raging. Arngeir agreed to host a peace council at High Hrothgar. With Esbern's help, I persuaded General Tullius and Ulfric Stormcloak to agree to a truce in order to defeat Alduin, who is a threat to both sides in the civil war.
translations.chinese: 白漫领主拒绝在内战持续的情况下帮助我捉龙。艾恩盖尔同意在霍斯加修道院主持和平会议。在伊思本的帮助下，我说服了图留斯将军和乌弗瑞克·风暴斗篷正视奥杜因的威胁，休战以击败他。

translations.english: The Greybeards summoned me to their monastery of High Hrothgar on the slopes of the Throat of the World. I spoke to Arngeir, one of the Greybeards, who believes that I am "Dragonborn", which means I am especially gifted in the use of the Voice. They have offered to help me learn to use my new abilities.
translations.chinese: 灰胡子将我召唤到了世界之喉高坡上的霍斯加修道院。我和灰胡子艾恩盖尔交谈。他相信我就是“龙裔”。这意味着我在吼声方面独具天赋。他们主动提出要帮我学习如何使用这个新能力。

translations.english: [pagebreak]
translations.chinese: [pagebreak]

translations.english: The Jarl of Whiterun won't help me capture a dragon as long as the civil war is raging. Arngeir has agreed to host a peace council at High Hrothgar. I need to convince the Imperials and the Stormcloaks to agree to a truce in order to defeat Alduin, who is a threat to both sides in the civil war.
translations.chinese: 白漫领主拒绝在内战持续的情况下帮助我捉龙。艾恩盖尔同意在霍斯加修道院主持和平会议。我需要说服帝国和风暴斗篷正视奥杜因的威胁，同意休战以将他击败。

translations.english: Press [Move] to walk or run, or [Jump] to jump. Press [Sprint] to sprint. Sprinting drains your stamina.
translations.chinese: 按下[Move]进行走或跑，[Jump]进行跳跃。按住[Sprint]即可进行冲刺。冲刺会消耗耐力。

translations.english: Press [Forward]/[Back]/[Strafe Left]/[Strafe Right] to walk or run, or [Jump] to jump. Press [Sprint] to sprint. Sprinting drains your stamina.
translations.chinese: 按[Forward]/[Back]/[Strafe Left]/[Strafe Right]来走动或跑步，或按[Jump]跳跃。按住[Sprint]进行冲刺。冲刺将消耗耐力。

translations.english: Absorb <20> magicka per second. If the target is out of magicka, absorb health instead.
translations.chinese: 每秒吸收<20>点魔力。如果目标魔力耗尽，则改为吸收生命值。

translations.english: +<mag> carrying capacity, and no movement penalty from armor.
translations.chinese: 负重增加<mag>点,不受护甲的移动惩罚。

translations.english: Press [Sprint] to sprint. Sprinting will drain your stamina.
translations.chinese: 按住[Sprint]进行冲刺。冲刺会消耗耐力。

translations.english: This reptilian race, well-suited for the treacherous swamps of their Black Marsh homeland, has developed a natural resistance to diseases and the ability to breathe underwater. They can call upon the Histskin to regenerate health very quickly.
translations.chinese: 身为有类爬行动物的种族，阿尔贡非常适应他们家乡黑沼泽的那些致命湿地，并发展出了一种天生的疾病抗性以及水下呼吸能力。他们可以使用“希斯特之皮”来促使生命快速恢复。

translations.english:
[pagebreak]

Third Era An Abbreviated Timeline by Jaspus Ignateous

[pagebreak]

t has been said that "citizens of the Empire who make the same mistakes as their forebears deserve to suffer the same fate." And while this may be true, it's hard to deny that the Empire's history is so long, and our forebears have made so many mistakes, it's sometimes hard to keep track. This work is meant to serve as a concise compilation of the Empire's most recent events, in this, our current age - what we refer to as the Third Era. It is a period of time that has as yet comprised less than five hundred years. But it should at least serve as a starting point for those who wish to study our Empire's vast and varied history. And maybe, just maybe, prevent the repeat of a previous disaster. It is also worth noting that when viewed in such a succinct structure, one truly gets a sense of just how often our great Empire has changed leadership. Indeed, it can be argued that much of the Empire's history in these past five centuries is the changing rule of that very Empire itself. First Century - 3E 0 - Beginning of Third Era, when all provinces in Tamriel are unified - 3E 38 - Death of Emperor Tiber Septim, and crowning of Emperor Pelagius - 3E 41 - Assassination of Emperor Pelagius, and crowning of Empress Kintyra - 3E 48 - Death of Empress Kintyra, and crowning of Emperor Uriel I - 3E 64 - Death of Emperor Uriel I, and crowning of Emperor Uriel II - 3E 82 - Death of Emperor Uriel II, and crowning of Emperor Pelagius II - 3E 99 - Death of Emperor Pelagius II, and crowning of Emperor Antiochus Second Century - 3E 110 - War of the Isle - 3E 111 - Knights of the Nine founded by Sir Amiel Lannus - 3E 114 - Reported death of Empress Kintyra II - 3E 119 - Birth of Pelagius III - 3E 121 - Uriel III Proclaimed Emperor - 3E 121 - War of the Red Diamond - 3E 123, 23 Frostfall - Actual death of Empress Kintyra II, in captivity, in secret - 3E 127 - Death of Emperor Uriel III, and crowning of Emperor Cephorus I - 3E 137 - Death of Potema, the Queen of Solitude - 3E 140 - Death of Emperor Cephorus I, and crowning of Emperor Magnus - 3E 145 - Death of Emperor Magnus, and crowning of Emperor Pelagius III - 3E 153 - Death of Emperor Pelagius III - 3E 153 - Katariah takes throne from husband Pelagius, becoming Empress Third Century - 3E 200 - Death of Empress Katariah, and crowning of Emperor Cassynder - 3E 202 - Death of Emperor Cassynder, and crowning of Emperor Uriel IV - 3E 247 - Death of Emperor Uriel IV, and crowning of Emperor Cephorus II - 3E 249 - Invasion of the Empire by the lich, Camoran Usurper - 3E 253 - Camoran Usurper controls the Dwynnen Region with "Nightmare Host" - 3E 267 - Defeat of Camoran Usurper - 3E 268 - Crowning of Emperor Uriel V - 3E 271-3E 284 - Various Conquests of Emperor Uriel Septim V - 3E 288 - Invasion of Akavir by the forces of Emperor Uriel Septim V - 3E 290 - Death of Emperor Uriel V, and crowning of Emperor Uriel VI Fourth Century - 3E 307 - Uriel VI gains full power as Emperor - 3E 320 - Death of Uriel VI, and crowning of Empress Morihatha - 3E 331 - Publication of the second edition of "A Pocket Guide to The Empire" - 3E 339 - Assassination of Empress Morihatha, and crowning of Emperor Pelagius IV - 3E 389 - Jagar Tharn betrays Emperor Uriel Septim VII - 3E 396 - Regional Wars Throughout Tamriel - 3E 396 - The Arnesian War - 3E 399 - Defeat of Jagar Tharn - 3E 399 - The founding of Orsinium Fifth Century - 3E 403 - Assassination of Lysandus, the King of Daggerfall - 3E 414 - Vvardenfell Territory opened for settlement - 3E 417 - The "Warp in the West" occurs - 3E 421 - Greywyn founds the Crimson Scars. - 3E 427 - Beginning of the Blight Curse in Vvardenfell, and arrival of the Nerevarine - 3E 427 - The Bloodmoon Prophecy comes to pass, on the isle of Solstheim - 3E 432 - Publication of the third edition of "A Pocket Guide to The Empire" - 3E 433 - Assassination of Emperor Uriel Septim VII - 3E 433 - The "Oblivion Crisis" - 3E 433 - The Knights of the Nine are reformed

第三纪元 简明时间轴 贾斯帕斯·伊格纳修斯 著

[pagebreak]

有人说，“帝国公民若犯下与祖先相同的错误，则应蒙受与之相同的命运。”虽然这话可能不假，但很难否认帝国历史如此悠久，我们的祖先犯下的错误也如此之多，有时已很难追溯了。 本作旨在为帝国近期大事进行简明汇编，在此，我们当前的时代——是被我们称作“第三纪元”的时代。虽然这一纪元始末还不足五百年。但对于那些想要研究我们帝国浩瀚多变历史者而言，它至少应该成为一个起点。也许，仅是也许，能防止过往的灾祸重演。 另值得注意的是，由这样简洁的结构观之，就能真正了解我们伟大帝国的统治层更迭之频繁。的确，可以说在过去五个世纪里，帝国的大部分历史就是帝国自身统治的变迁。 第一世纪 · 3E0年 – 第三纪元开始，泰姆瑞尔所有行省统一 · 3E38年 -泰伯·塞普汀皇帝去世，佩拉吉奥斯皇帝加冕 · 3E41年 - 佩拉吉奥斯皇帝遇刺，金泰拉女皇加冕 · 3E48年 - 金泰拉女皇去世，尤瑞尔一世皇帝加冕 · 3E64年 - 尤瑞尔一世皇帝去世，尤瑞尔二世皇帝加冕 · 3E82年 - 尤瑞尔二世皇帝去世，佩拉吉奥斯二世皇帝加冕 · 3E99年 - 佩拉吉奥斯二世皇帝去世，安条克皇帝加冕 第二世纪 · 3E110年 - 岛屿之战 · 3E111年 - 阿米尔·兰努斯爵士建立九圣灵骑士团 · 3E114年 – 据称金泰拉二世女皇去世 · 3E119年 - 佩拉吉奥斯三世降生 · 3E121年 - 尤瑞尔三世宣称继任皇帝 · 3E121年 - 红钻战争爆发 · 3E123年霜落月23日 - 金泰拉二世女皇于秘密监禁死亡 · 3E127年 - 尤瑞尔三世皇帝去世，塞弗鲁斯一世皇帝加冕 · 3E137年 - 独孤太后波特玛去世 · 3E140年 - 塞弗鲁斯一世皇帝去世，玛格努斯皇帝加冕 · 3E145年 - 玛格努斯皇帝去世，佩拉吉奥斯三世皇帝加冕 · 3E153年 - 佩拉吉奥斯三世皇帝去世 · 3E153年 - 卡塔瑞亚接替丈夫佩拉吉奥斯，进位女皇 第三世纪 · 3E200年 - 卡塔瑞亚女皇去世，卡辛德皇帝继位 · 3E202年 - 卡辛德皇帝去世，尤瑞尔四世皇帝加冕 · 3E247年 - 尤瑞尔四世皇帝去世，塞弗鲁斯二世皇帝加冕 · 3E249年 – 巫妖卡莫然篡位者侵略帝国 · 3E253年 - 卡莫然篡位者凭“梦魇军团”控制杜维能地区 · 3E267年 - 卡莫然篡位者被击败 · 3E268年 - 尤瑞尔五世皇帝加冕 · 3E271-284年 - 尤瑞尔·塞普汀五世皇帝开始多次征服战争 · 3E288年 - 尤瑞尔·塞普汀五世皇帝率领大军远征阿卡维尔 · 3E290年 - 尤瑞尔五世皇帝去世，尤瑞尔六世皇帝加冕 第四世纪 · 3E307年 - 尤瑞尔六世获得作为皇帝的全部权力 · 3E320年 - 尤瑞尔六世皇帝去世，莫里哈萨女皇加冕 · 3E331年 - 《袖珍帝国指南》第二版出版 · 3E339年 -莫里哈萨女皇遇刺，佩拉吉奥斯四世皇帝加冕 · 3E389年 – 贾伽·萨恩背叛尤瑞尔·塞普汀七世皇帝 · 3E396年 – 全泰姆瑞尔各地爆发局部战争 · 3E396年 – 阿尼西亚战争爆发 · 3E399年- 贾伽·萨恩被击破 · 3E399年 - 奥辛纽姆建立 第五世纪 · 3E403年 - 匕落国王莱桑杜斯遇刺 · 3E414年- 瓦登费尔领地开放供定居 · 3E417年 - “西方扭曲”发生 · 3E421年 - 格雷温建立“猩红创伤” · 3E427年 - 瓦登费尔开始出现枯萎病诅咒，尼瑞瓦因现世 · 3E427年 - 血月预言在索瑟海姆岛实现 · 3E432年 - 《袖珍帝国指南》第三版出版 · 3E433年 - 尤瑞尔·塞普汀七世遇刺 · 3E433年 – “湮灭危机” · 3E433年 - 九圣灵骑士团重建

Third Era An Abbreviated Timeline by Jaspus Ignateous

[pagebreak]

t has been said that "citizens of the Empire who make the same mistakes as their forebears deserve to suffer the same fate." And while this may be true, it's hard to deny that the Empire's history is so long, and our forebears have made so many mistakes, it's sometimes hard to keep track. This work is meant to serve as a concise compilation of the Empire's most recent events, in this, our current age - what we refer to as the Third Era. It is a period of time that has as yet comprised less than five hundred years. But it should at least serve as a starting point for those who wish to study our Empire's vast and varied history. And maybe, just maybe, prevent the repeat of a previous disaster. It is also worth noting that when viewed in such a succinct structure, one truly gets a sense of just how often our great Empire has changed leadership. Indeed, it can be argued that much of the Empire's history in these past five centuries is the changing rule of that very Empire itself. First Century - 3E 0 - Beginning of Third Era, when all provinces in Tamriel are unified - 3E 38 - Death of Emperor Tiber Septim, and crowning of Emperor Pelagius - 3E 41 - Assassination of Emperor Pelagius, and crowning of Empress Kintyra - 3E 48 - Death of Empress Kintyra, and crowning of Emperor Uriel I - 3E 64 - Death of Emperor Uriel I, and crowning of Emperor Uriel II - 3E 82 - Death of Emperor Uriel II, and crowning of Emperor Pelagius II - 3E 99 - Death of Emperor Pelagius II, and crowning of Emperor Antiochus Second Century - 3E 110 - War of the Isle - 3E 111 - Knights of the Nine founded by Sir Amiel Lannus - 3E 114 - Reported death of Empress Kintyra II - 3E 119 - Birth of Pelagius III - 3E 121 - Uriel III Proclaimed Emperor - 3E 121 - War of the Red Diamond - 3E 123, 23 Frostfall - Actual death of Empress Kintyra II, in captivity, in secret - 3E 127 - Death of Emperor Uriel III, and crowning of Emperor Cephorus I - 3E 137 - Death of Potema, the Queen of Solitude - 3E 140 - Death of Emperor Cephorus I, and crowning of Emperor Magnus - 3E 145 - Death of Emperor Magnus, and crowning of Emperor Pelagius III - 3E 153 - Death of Emperor Pelagius III - 3E 153 - Katariah takes throne from husband Pelagius, becoming Empress Third Century - 3E 200 - Death of Empress Katariah, and crowning of Emperor Cassynder - 3E 202 - Death of Emperor Cassynder, and crowning of Emperor Uriel IV - 3E 247 - Death of Emperor Uriel IV, and crowning of Emperor Cephorus II - 3E 249 - Invasion of the Empire by the lich, Camoran Usurper - 3E 253 - Camoran Usurper controls the Dwynnen Region with "Nightmare Host" - 3E 267 - Defeat of Camoran Usurper - 3E 268 - Crowning of Emperor Uriel V - 3E 271-3E 284 - Various Conquests of Emperor Uriel Septim V - 3E 288 - Invasion of Akavir by the forces of Emperor Uriel Septim V - 3E 290 - Death of Emperor Uriel V, and crowning of Emperor Uriel VI Fourth Century - 3E 307 - Uriel VI gains full power as Emperor - 3E 320 - Death of Uriel VI, and crowning of Empress Morihatha - 3E 331 - Publication of the second edition of "A Pocket Guide to The Empire" - 3E 339 - Assassination of Empress Morihatha, and crowning of Emperor Pelagius IV - 3E 389 - Jagar Tharn betrays Emperor Uriel Septim VII - 3E 396 - Regional Wars Throughout Tamriel - 3E 396 - The Arnesian War - 3E 399 - Defeat of Jagar Tharn - 3E 399 - The founding of Orsinium Fifth Century - 3E 403 - Assassination of Lysandus, the King of Daggerfall - 3E 414 - Vvardenfell Territory opened for settlement - 3E 417 - The "Warp in the West" occurs - 3E 421 - Greywyn founds the Crimson Scars. - 3E 427 - Beginning of the Blight Curse in Vvardenfell, and arrival of the Nerevarine - 3E 427 - The Bloodmoon Prophecy comes to pass, on the isle of Solstheim - 3E 432 - Publication of the third edition of "A Pocket Guide to The Empire" - 3E 433 - Assassination of Emperor Uriel Septim VII - 3E 433 - The "Oblivion Crisis" - 3E 433 - The Knights of the Nine are reformed | ZH: [pagebreak]

The Argonian Account Book Two By Waughin Jarth

[pagebreak]

ecumus Scotti emerged from the dirt and reeds, exhausted from running, his face and arms sheathed in red fleshflies. Looking back towards Cyrodiil, he saw the bridge disappear beneath the thick black river, and he knew he was not getting back until the tide went down in a few days' time. The river also held in its adhesive depths his files on the Black Marsh account. He would have to rely on his memory for his contacts in Gideon. Mailic was purposefully striding through the reeds ahead. Flailing ineffectually at the fleshflies, Scotti hurried after him. "We're lucky, sir," said the Redguard, which struck Scotti as an extraordinarily odd thing to say, until his eyes followed where the man's finger was pointing. "The caravan is here." Twenty-one rusted, mud-spattered wagons with rotting wood and wobbly wheels sat half-sunk in the soft earth ahead. A crowd of Argonians, gray-scaled and gray-eyed, the sort of sullen manual laborers that were common in Cyrodiil, pulled at one of the wagons which had been detached from the others. As Scotti and Mailic came closer, they saw it was filled with a cargo of black berries so decayed that they had become hardly recognizable... more a festering jelly than a wagonload of fruit. Yes, they were going to the city of Gideon, and, yes, they said, Scotti could get a ride with them after they were finished unloading this shipment of lumberries. "How long ago were they picked?" Scotti asked, looking at the wagon's rotten produce. "The harvest was in Last Seed, of course," said the Argonian who seemed to be in charge of the wagon. It was now Sun's Dusk, so they had been en route from the fields for a little over two months. Clearly, Scotti thought, there were problems with transportation. But fixing that, after all, was what he was doing here as a representative of Lord Vanech's Building Commission. It took close to an hour of the berries rotting even more in the sun for the wagon to be pushed to the side, the wagons in front of it and behind it to be attached to one another, and one of the eight horses from the front of the caravan to be brought around to the now independent wagon. The laborers moved with dispirited lethargy, and Scotti took the opportunity to inspect the rest of the caravan and talk to his fellow travellers. Four of the wagons had benches in them, fit for uncomfortable riders. All the rest were filled with grain, meat, and vegetation in various stages of corruption. The travellers consisted of the six Argonian laborers, three Imperial merchants so bug-bitten that their skin looked as scaly as the Argonians themselves, and three cloaked fellows who were evidently Dunmer, judging by the red eyes that gleamed in the shadows under their hoods. All were transporting their goods along this, the Imperial Commerce Road. "This is a road?" Scotti exclaimed, looking at the endless field of reeds that reached up to his chin or higher. "It's solid ground, of a sort," one of the hooded Dunmer shrugged. "The horses eat some of the reed, and sometimes we set fire to it, but it just grows right back up." Finally, the wagonmaster signalled that the caravan was ready to go, and Scotti took a seat in the third wagon with the other Imperials. He looked around, but Mailic was not on board. "I agreed to get to you to Black Marsh and take you back out," said the Redguard, who had plumped down a rock in the sea of reeds and was munching on a hairy carrot. "I'll be here when you get back." Scotti frowned, and not only because Mailic had dropped the deferential title "sir" while addressing him. Now he truly knew no one in Black Marsh, but the caravan slowly grinded and bumped forward, so there was no time to argue. A noxious wind blew across the Commerce Road, casting patterns in the endless featureless expanse of reeds. In the distance, there seemed to be mountains, but they constantly shifted, and Scotti realized they were banks of mist and fog. Shadows flitted across the landscape, and when Scotti looked up, he saw they were being cast by giant birds with long, saw-like beaks nearly the size of the rest of their bodies. "Hackwings," Chaero Gemullus, an Imperial on Scotti's left, who might have been young but looked old and beaten, muttered. "Like everything else in this damnable place, they'll eat you if you don't keep moving. Beggars pounce down and give you a nasty chop, and then fly off and come back when you're mostly dead from blood loss." Scotti shivered. He hoped they'd be in Gideon before nightfall. It was then it occurred to him that the sun was on the wrong side of the caravan. "Excuse me, sir," Scotti called to the wagonmaster. "I thought you said we were going to Gideon?" The wagonmaster nodded. "Why are we going north then, when we should be going south?" There was no reply but a sigh. Scotti confirmed with his fellow travellers that they too were going to Gideon, and none of them seemed very concerned about the circuitous route to getting there. The seats were hard on his middle-aged back and buttocks, but the bumping rhythm of the caravan, and the hypnotic waving reeds gradually had an effect on him, and Scotti drifted off to sleep. He awoke in the dark some hours later, not sure where he was. The caravan was no longer moving, and he was on the floor, under the bench, next to some small boxes. There were voices, speaking a hissing, clicking language Scotti didn't understand, and he peeked out between someone's legs to see what was happening. The moons barely pierced the thick mist surrounding the caravan, and Scotti did not have the best angle to see who was talking. For a moment, it looked like the gray wagonmaster was talking to himself, but the darkness had movement and moisture, in fact, glistening scales. It was hard to tell how many of these things there were, but they were big, black, and the more Scotti looked at them, the more details he could see. When one particular detail emerged, huge mouths filled with dripping needle-like fangs, Scotti slipped back under the bench. Their black little eyes had not fallen on him yet. The legs in front of Scotti moved and then began to thrash, as their owner was grabbed and pulled out of the wagon. Scotti crouched further back, getting behind the little boxes. He didn't know much about concealment, but had some experience with shields. He knew that having something, anything, in between you and bad things was always good. A few seconds after the legs had disappeared from sight, there was a horrible scream. And then a second and a third. Different timbres, different accents, but the same inarticulate message... terror, and pain, horrible pain. Scotti remembered a long forgotten prayer to the god Stendarr and whispered it to himself. Then there was silence... ghastly silence that lasted only a few minutes, but which seemed like hours... years. And then the carriage started rolling forward again. Scotti cautiously crawled out from under the carriage. Chaero Gemullus gave him a bemused grin. "There you are," he said. "I thought the Nagas took you." "Nagas?" "Nasty characters," Gemullus said, frowning. "Puff adders with legs and arms, seven feet tall, eight when they're mad. Come from the inner swamp, and they don't like it here much so they're particularly peevish. You're the kind of posh Imperial they're looking for." Scotti had never in his life thought of himself as posh. His mud and fleshfly-bespeckled clothing seemed eminently middle-class, at best, to him. "What would they want me for?" "To rob, of course," the Imperial smiled. "And to kill. You didn't notice what happened to the others?" The Imperial frowned, as if struck by a thought. "You didn't sample from those boxes down below, did you? Like the sugar, do you?" "Gods, no," Scotti grimaced. The Imperial nodded, relieved. "You just seem a little slow. First time to Black Marsh, I gather? Oh! Heigh ho, Hist piss!" Scotti was just about to ask Gemullus what that vulgar term meant when the rain began. It was an inferno of foul-smelling, yellow-brown rain that washed over the caravan, accompanied by the growl of thunder in the distance. Gemullus worked to pull the roof up over the wagon, glaring at Scotti until he helped with the laborious process. He shuddered, not only from the cold damp, but from contemplation of the disgusting precipitation pouring down on the already nasty produce in the uncovered wagon. "We'll be dry soon enough," Gemullus smiled, pointing out into the fog. Scotti had never been to Gideon, but he knew what to expect. A large settlement more or less laid out like a Imperial city, with more or less Imperial style architecture, and all the Imperial comforts and traditions, more or less. The jumble of huts half-sunk in mud was decidedly less. "Where are we?" asked Scotti, bewildered. "Hixinoag," replied Gemullus, pronouncing the queer name with confidence. "You were right. We were going north when we should have been going south."
translations.chinese:
[pagebreak]

阿尔贡合同 卷二 瓦辛·贾斯 著

[pagebreak]

戴奎莫斯·斯科提从泥沼和芦苇间爬出来，跑得筋疲力尽，他的脸上和手臂上覆满了红色的肉蝇。他回头望向希罗帝尔，桥已隐没在黑沉浊流中，他知道除非潮水在几天后退下，否则他是回不去了。河水也把他的黑沼泽事务文件留在了那粘稠深处。他只能依靠自己的记忆找到吉迪昂的联系人了。 梅耶里克果断地在芦苇丛中开路。斯科提徒劳地驱赶着肉蝇，紧紧跟在他后面。 “我们很走运，先生。”红卫人说道。这话让斯科提觉得很奇怪，直到他看向男人手指所指之处。“有车队来了。” 二十一辆锈迹斑斑、溅满泥土的货车出现在前方，车体朽烂，轮子摇晃，半没在前方的软土里。一帮灰鳞片灰眼睛的阿尔贡——希罗帝尔常见的那种阴沉的劳工——拉着一辆接一辆的货车。当斯科提和梅耶里克走近时，才看到货车里装满了腐烂到难以辨认的黑莓……不如说是溃烂的果酱。 好在，他们要去吉迪昂，更好的是，他们同意在卸完货后顺路捎斯科提一程。 “它们被采下来多久了？”斯科提看着货车里腐烂的货物，问道。 “当然是在末种月收的。”一个看起来像是车队领队的阿尔贡回答。现在已是日暮月，它们肯定从田里运出来超过两个月了。 斯科提认为显然是运输方法出了问题。但话说回来，解决这些问题正是他作为瓦内奇建筑委员会代表来到这里的目的。 花了将近一小时，车辆才被推到一边，车上的黑莓被暴晒的越发腐烂。货车首尾相连，最前面的车由八匹马拉着，其中的一匹马现在还被带到了一辆独立的车上。劳工们无精打采地行进着，斯科提便趁此机会去看看其他的蓬车，并与同行者聊聊天。 四辆货车里都有长条凳，能让感到不适的乘客休息一下。其余的车里装满了谷物、肉、蔬菜，腐坏程度不一。 旅客包括六名阿尔贡劳工，三名被虫叮得皮肤像阿尔贡人鳞片般的帝国商人，还有三个披斗篷的家伙，从他们兜帽阴影下闪烁的红色眼睛判断，显然是丹莫人。他们都是沿着这条帝国商路运输货物的。 “这也叫路？”斯科提感叹着看向外面一望无际，长满长及下颌的芦苇丛道。 “至少地面还算结实。”戴兜帽的丹莫耸了耸肩，“马会吃掉一些芦苇，有时我们会放火烧，但你一转身它又长起来了。” 最后，车队头领示意车队已经可以行进了，斯科提便与其他的帝国人坐在第三辆货车上。他环顾四周，梅耶里克已不在车上。 “我答应带你到黑沼泽并带你回去，”红卫人说，然后他一屁股坐在芦苇海中的一块石头上，拿出一块多须的胡萝卜边啃边说，“我就在这等你回来。” 斯科提皱了皱眉，不仅仅是因为梅耶里克在称呼他时没有用敬语“先生”。现在他在黑沼泽没有认识的人了。但车队已经缓缓颠簸向前，没时间再争论了。 一阵难闻的风掠过商路，在毫无特色、一望无际的芦苇地上吹出了各种图案。远方看起来好像有群山，但它们不断移动，斯科提意识到那不过是一团薄雾而已。影子飞快地掠过大地，斯科蒂抬头一看，发现原来是一些巨鸟，它们长着锯子般的长喙，几乎和身体的其他部分一样长。 “是割翼鸟。”查罗·杰姆卢斯小声嘟囔道，这个斯科蒂左边的帝国人可能还很年轻却看上去又老又憔悴。抱怨道。“就像这该死地方的其他玩意，如果你不跑它们就会吃掉你。这帮混蛋扑下来偷袭一口就飞走，等你快因失血而死时再飞回来。” 斯科蒂打了个哆嗦。他希望他们能在天黑前赶到吉迪昂。就在这时，他突然发现太阳在商队的错误方向。 “打扰了，先生，”斯科提对车主说道，“我记得你说我们是去吉迪昂？” 车主点了点头。 “那为什么我们向北走呢，难道不应该往南走？” 除了一声叹息外别无答复。 斯科提确认他的旅伴也是要去吉迪昂，但他们似乎并不关心这个绕行的路线会到哪里。对于一个像他这样中年人的背部和臀部来说，这凳子太硬了，但篷车颠簸的节奏，还有催眠般的芦苇摇摆渐渐影响了他，斯科提睡着了。 几小时后他在黑暗中醒来，不知道自己身处何处。篷车不再移动，他感觉自己在长条凳下的地板上，靠近一些小箱子。斯科提听到一些嘶嘶咔咔的声音，他听不懂，于是他从某人的腿缝间窥视外面发生了什么。 月光勉强穿透了车队周围的浓雾，斯科提的角度不太合适，看不清是谁在讲话。有一会儿，似乎是那个灰色的车夫在自言自语，但黑暗与水汽中有东西在移动，实际上，那是反光的鳞片。很难辨别这些生物到底有多少，但他们又大又黑，并且斯科提观察得越多，就能看到更多的细节。 这时一个特别的细节出现了，斯科提看到了一张湿漉漉的长满尖牙的大嘴，他赶紧缩回到长凳底下。幸好那些黑色的小眼睛没有注意到他。 斯科提前的一双腿开始剧烈摆动，腿的主人被抓起来并扔到了车外面。斯科提更加向后蜷缩，躲在一个小箱子后。他不太懂如何隐藏自己，但有一些用盾的经验。他知道，在自己和危险之间有个什么东西，不管是什么，总归是好的。 在那双腿从视野中消失几秒钟后，传来一声惨叫。接着传来第二声第三声。不同的腔调，不同的口音，但传递着同种难以言喻的讯息……恐惧、疼痛、极度的痛苦。斯科蒂记起了一段遗忘的斯丹达尔祷告，小声念叨起来。 之后是一片寂静……恐怖的寂静只持续了几分钟，可感觉就像几小时，或者说，几年…… 车队再一次驶向前去。 斯科提小心翼翼地爬出篷车。查罗·杰姆卢斯给了他一个困惑地笑容。 “原来你在这里，”他说，“我还以为娜迦把你抓走了。” “娜迦？” “恶毒的生物，”杰姆卢斯皱着眉说。“有手有脚的蝰蛇，有七尺高，发狂时有八尺。他们来自沼泽深处，显然他们不喜欢这里，因此变得特别易怒。你正是他们要找的那种上流帝国人。” 斯科提从未想过可以用上流这个词形容自己。对他来说，他风尘仆仆、沾满肉蝇的衣服充其量是典型的中产者。“他们要我做什么？” “当然是抢劫，”帝国人笑了。“然后杀掉。你没注意到发生在其他人身上的事吗？”帝国人突然皱了眉，好像突然想到了什么。“你没吃下面那些箱子里的东西吧？比如说糖之类的？” “谢天谢地，没有。”斯科提扮了个鬼脸。 帝国人点了点头，放心了。“你只是看起来有点呆。我看你是第一次去黑沼泽？靠！希斯特撒尿了！” 斯科提刚想问杰姆卢斯那句粗话是什么意思，雨就下起来了。地狱般腥臭黄褐的雨冲刷着篷车，伴随着远方雷电的咆哮声。杰姆卢斯开始支起货车的顶棚，边做边盯着斯科提，直到他也加入到这体力劳动中。 他发抖了，不仅仅因为寒冷的湿气，还因为看着这恶心的雨水落到那些没遮盖的货车里已经烂透了的农产品上。 “很快就干了，”杰姆卢斯笑着指向外面的雾。 斯科提从没去过吉迪昂，但他也知道会发生什么。一座多少类似帝国城市的大型聚落，多少带有一些帝国建筑风格，也会有些帝国的舒适与传统。 那些半陷入泥沼的杂乱小屋明显少了许多。 “我们在哪？”斯科提困惑地问。 “希辛阿格，”杰姆卢斯回答，自信的说出这个拗口的名字。“你是对的。我们本应该朝南去的，可是现在我们正往北。”

The Argonian Account Book Two By Waughin Jarth

[pagebreak]

ecumus Scotti emerged from the dirt and reeds, exhausted from running, his face and arms sheathed in red fleshflies. Looking back towards Cyrodiil, he saw the bridge disappear beneath the thick black river, and he knew he was not getting back until the tide went down in a few days' time. The river also held in its adhesive depths his files on the Black Marsh account. He would have to rely on his memory for his contacts in Gideon. Mailic was purposefully striding through the reeds ahead. Flailing ineffectually at the fleshflies, Scotti hurried after him. "We're lucky, sir," said the Redguard, which struck Scotti as an extraordinarily odd thing to say, until his eyes followed where the man's finger was pointing. "The caravan is here." Twenty-one rusted, mud-spattered wagons with rotting wood and wobbly wheels sat half-sunk in the soft earth ahead. A crowd of Argonians, gray-scaled and gray-eyed, the sort of sullen manual laborers that were common in Cyrodiil, pulled at one of the wagons which had been detached from the others. As Scotti and Mailic came closer, they saw it was filled with a cargo of black berries so decayed that they had become hardly recognizable... more a festering jelly than a wagonload of fruit. Yes, they were going to the city of Gideon, and, yes, they said, Scotti could get a ride with them after they were finished unloading this shipment of lumberries. "How long ago were they picked?" Scotti asked, looking at the wagon's rotten produce. "The harvest was in Last Seed, of course," said the Argonian who seemed to be in charge of the wagon. It was now Sun's Dusk, so they had been en route from the fields for a little over two months. Clearly, Scotti thought, there were problems with transportation. But fixing that, after all, was what he was doing here as a representative of Lord Vanech's Building Commission. It took close to an hour of the berries rotting even more in the sun for the wagon to be pushed to the side, the wagons in front of it and behind it to be attached to one another, and one of the eight horses from the front of the caravan to be brought around to the now independent wagon. The laborers moved with dispirited lethargy, and Scotti took the opportunity to inspect the rest of the caravan and talk to his fellow travellers. Four of the wagons had benches in them, fit for uncomfortable riders. All the rest were filled with grain, meat, and vegetation in various stages of corruption. The travellers consisted of the six Argonian laborers, three Imperial merchants so bug-bitten that their skin looked as scaly as the Argonians themselves, and three cloaked fellows who were evidently Dunmer, judging by the red eyes that gleamed in the shadows under their hoods. All were transporting their goods along this, the Imperial Commerce Road. "This is a road?" Scotti exclaimed, looking at the endless field of reeds that reached up to his chin or higher. "It's solid ground, of a sort," one of the hooded Dunmer shrugged. "The horses eat some of the reed, and sometimes we set fire to it, but it just grows right back up." Finally, the wagonmaster signalled that the caravan was ready to go, and Scotti took a seat in the third wagon with the other Imperials. He looked around, but Mailic was not on board. "I agreed to get to you to Black Marsh and take you back out," said the Redguard, who had plumped down a rock in the sea of reeds and was munching on a hairy carrot. "I'll be here when you get back." Scotti frowned, and not only because Mailic had dropped the deferential title "sir" while addressing him. Now he truly knew no one in Black Marsh, but the caravan slowly grinded and bumped forward, so there was no time to argue. A noxious wind blew across the Commerce Road, casting patterns in the endless featureless expanse of reeds. In the distance, there seemed to be mountains, but they constantly shifted, and Scotti realized they were banks of mist and fog. Shadows flitted across the landscape, and when Scotti looked up, he saw they were being cast by giant birds with long, saw-like beaks nearly the size of the rest of their bodies. "Hackwings," Chaero Gemullus, an Imperial on Scotti's left, who might have been young but looked old and beaten, muttered. "Like everything else in this damnable place, they'll eat you if you don't keep moving. Beggars pounce down and give you a nasty chop, and then fly off and come back when you're mostly dead from blood loss." Scotti shivered. He hoped they'd be in Gideon before nightfall. It was then it occurred to him that the sun was on the wrong side of the caravan. "Excuse me, sir," Scotti called to the wagonmaster. "I thought you said we were going to Gideon?" The wagonmaster nodded. "Why are we going north then, when we should be going south?" There was no reply but a sigh. Scotti confirmed with his fellow travellers that they too were going to Gideon, and none of them seemed very concerned about the circuitous route to getting there. The seats were hard on his middle-aged back and buttocks, but the bumping rhythm of the caravan, and the hypnotic waving reeds gradually had an effect on him, and Scotti drifted off to sleep. He awoke in the dark some hours later, not sure where he was. The caravan was no longer moving, and he was on the floor, under the bench, next to some small boxes. There were voices, speaking a hissing, clicking language Scotti didn't understand, and he peeked out between someone's legs to see what was happening. The moons barely pierced the thick mist surrounding the caravan, and Scotti did not have the best angle to see who was talking. For a moment, it looked like the gray wagonmaster was talking to himself, but the darkness had movement and moisture, in fact, glistening scales. It was hard to tell how many of these things there were, but they were big, black, and the more Scotti looked at them, the more details he could see. When one particular detail emerged, huge mouths filled with dripping needle-like fangs, Scotti slipped back under the bench. Their black little eyes had not fallen on him yet. The legs in front of Scotti moved and then began to thrash, as their owner was grabbed and pulled out of the wagon. Scotti crouched further back, getting behind the little boxes. He didn't know much about concealment, but had some experience with shields. He knew that having something, anything, in between you and bad things was always good. A few seconds after the legs had disappeared from sight, there was a horrible scream. And then a second and a third. Different timbres, different accents, but the same inarticulate message... terror, and pain, horrible pain. Scotti remembered a long forgotten prayer to the god Stendarr and whispered it to himself. Then there was silence... ghastly silence that lasted only a few minutes, but which seemed like hours... years. And then the carriage started rolling forward again. Scotti cautiously crawled out from under the carriage. Chaero Gemullus gave him a bemused grin. "There you are," he said. "I thought the Nagas took you." "Nagas?" "Nasty characters," Gemullus said, frowning. "Puff adders with legs and arms, seven feet tall, eight when they're mad. Come from the inner swamp, and they don't like it here much so they're particularly peevish. You're the kind of posh Imperial they're looking for." Scotti had never in his life thought of himself as posh. His mud and fleshfly-bespeckled clothing seemed eminently middle-class, at best, to him. "What would they want me for?" "To rob, of course," the Imperial smiled. "And to kill. You didn't notice what happened to the others?" The Imperial frowned, as if struck by a thought. "You didn't sample from those boxes down below, did you? Like the sugar, do you?" "Gods, no," Scotti grimaced. The Imperial nodded, relieved. "You just seem a little slow. First time to Black Marsh, I gather? Oh! Heigh ho, Hist piss!" Scotti was just about to ask Gemullus what that vulgar term meant when the rain began. It was an inferno of foul-smelling, yellow-brown rain that washed over the caravan, accompanied by the growl of thunder in the distance. Gemullus worked to pull the roof up over the wagon, glaring at Scotti until he helped with the laborious process. He shuddered, not only from the cold damp, but from contemplation of the disgusting precipitation pouring down on the already nasty produce in the uncovered wagon. "We'll be dry soon enough," Gemullus smiled, pointing out into the fog. Scotti had never been to Gideon, but he knew what to expect. A large settlement more or less laid out like a Imperial city, with more or less Imperial style architecture, and all the Imperial comforts and traditions, more or less. The jumble of huts half-sunk in mud was decidedly less. "Where are we?" asked Scotti, bewildered. "Hixinoag," replied Gemullus, pronouncing the queer name with confidence. "You were right. We were going north when we should have been going south." | ZH: [pagebreak]

阿尔贡合同 卷二 瓦辛·贾斯 著

[pagebreak]
